title: The Playground
mainIdea: Molly solved a problem with her friend Bella at the playground by using an "I message" to express her feelings so they could properly take turns on the swing.

pages.text: Molly and her friend Bella were playing at the playground. They were supposed to take turns on the swing. Molly counted up to 100 while Bella was on the swing.
Bella didn't go down the swing. "Bella! I counted up to 100, it's my turn!" said Molly. "You counted too fast, it's still my turn," Bella said.
Molly was upset. She wanted a turn on the swing, too! What could she do? Molly could tell the teacher, but her teacher always wanted them to try to solve their problems first.

options: Bella would not give her a turn

pages.text: How would she solve the problem? She decided to use an "I message". That means to tell the other person how you're feeling, why you feel that way, and what they can do to make it better.

question.text: What did Molly decide to do?
options: Use an "I message" to share her feelings
Take the swing while Bella wasn't looking

pages.text: "Bella, I feel sad because we were supposed to take turns after 100 counts. Will you please get off?" Molly asked. Finally, Bella got off the swing. Molly was happy because she solved her problem all by herself!

options: Bella stayed on the swing all day
Bella got off and Molly had her turn

elements.correct: She wanted a turn on the swing
distractors: She wanted to go home early
She wanted a brand new swing

elements.correct: Bella would not get off the swing
Molly used an "I message" to tell Bella how she felt
distractors: Molly told the teacher right away
Molly walked away and gave up

elements.correct: Bella got off and Molly happily took her turn
distractors: The teacher gave them a new swing
